title: Vocabulary
objective: Students will explore vocabulary concepts including Formal and Informal English, Word Families and Root Words.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Formal and Informal English!
Recognise and compare formal and informal uses of English, understanding that language choices vary based on audience, purpose and context
Time to try this out: Identify whether a spoken or written example uses formal or informal language (e.g., 'Dear Sir' vs 'Hey mate')
Does you understand the difference between how they'd write a text to a friend versus a formal letter — and can they explain why the language would be different?

Great job! Today we learned about Formal and Informal English. Identify whether a spoken or written example uses formal or informal language (e.g., 'Dear Sir' vs 'Hey mate') Rewrite an informal sentence in a more formal way (e.g., change 'Can I have some?' to 'May I please have some?')
Let's discover the amazing world of Word Families and Root Words!

Explore word families based on common root words, understanding how words are related in form and meaning through shared roots, prefixes and suffixes (e.g., solve → solution, solver, dissolve, insoluble)
Time to try this out: Generate at least 4 words in a word family from a given root (e.g., from 'play': player, playful, replay, playground)
If you knows the word "cycle," can they work out what related words like "recycle," "bicycle," or "cyclist" mean by thinking about the shared root?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Word Families and Root Words. Generate at least 4 words in a word family from a given root (e.g., from 'play': player, playful, replay, playground) Explain how members of a word family are connected in meaning (e.g., 'solve, solution, dissolve all relate to finding answers or breaking apart')
